slides.title: The air indoors may be *quietly aging* you.
slides.sub: We spend most of our lives inside, where fine particles and stale air build up. Small fixes can change that.

slides.title: Indoor air is not neutral
slides.body: Cooking, candles and traffic leave behind *fine particles* called PM2.5. Indoors, they can climb higher than the air outside.

slides.title: Tiny particles travel far
slides.body: PM2.5 is small enough to reach the bloodstream. Long exposure *is linked to* strain on the heart, lungs and brain.

slides.title: Stale air dulls thinking
slides.body: In closed rooms, exhaled CO2 builds up fast. Higher levels *are linked to* slower focus and foggier decisions.

slides.title: Move the air
slides.body: Cracking a window for a few minutes clears built-up CO2. Fresh airflow *can support* clearer thinking through the day.

slides.title: Filter what you breathe
slides.body: A HEPA purifier traps most fine particles. Running one where you sleep *may help* protect your lungs over the long run.

slides.title: Care for the *invisible inputs*.
slides.body: Air is one input. Vibe Health tracks the rest: your sleep, food, movement and steps, rolled into one daily Health Score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The air you breathe at home shapes how you feel, focus and age. 🌿💨

Fine particles strain the body, stale air clouds the mind, and a window plus a HEPA purifier can quietly help both. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your daily habits into one simple score → link in bio.
